summary: This fragment defines the legal concept of collateral estoppel in the context of Civil Procedure.
key_points: Collateral estoppel is a fundamental concept in the US legal system.
The legal definition encompasses issue preclusion, mutuality, non-mutual offensive/defensive.
This concept is established through statutory law and judicial precedent.
Understanding this definition is essential for legal practitioners and scholars.
Key Supreme Court cases have refined the scope of collateral estoppel.
The concept serves as a foundation for related legal doctrines.

philosophy_guard.statement: This fragment describes what IS - the existing legal framework, procedures, and analytical perspectives. It does not advocate for what OUGHT to be.
